page.content: The physician should only physically examine the man if his semen analysis is irregular, classified as LOE C, according to the medical-technical recommendations for general infertility for physicians, specifically for family physician (physical examination). The gynecologist should not test ovarian reserve capacity to predict probability of pregnancy (with or without treatment), classified as LOE A, as outlined in the recommendations for unexplained infertility. Additionally, the physician should order a semen analysis from an accredited laboratory (ISO15189) or from a referral hospital, categorized as LOE D, under the organizational recommendations for the organization of fertility care.

lines.text: The physician should only physically examine the man if his semen analysis is irregular, classified as LOE C, according to the
medical-technical recommendations for general infertility for physicians, specifically for family physician (physical
examination). The gynecologist should not test ovarian reserve capacity to predict probability of pregnancy (with or without
treatment), classified as LOE A, as outlined in the recommendations for unexplained infertility. Additionally, the physician
should order a semen analysis from an accredited laboratory (ISO15189) or from a referral hospital, categorized as LOE D,
under the organizational recommendations for the organization of fertility care.

page.html:
Medical-technical recommendationsOrigin or guidelineChapter of the MuG
The physician should only physically examine the man if his semen analysis is irregular. LOE CGeneral infertility for physiciansFamily physician (physical examination)
The gynecologist should not test ovarian reserve capacity to predict probability of pregnancy (with or without treatment). LOE AUnexplained infertilityGynecologist (assessments)
Organizational recommendations
The physician should order a semen analysis from an accredited laboratory (ISO15189) or from a referral hospital. LOE DMuGOrganization of fertility care
In accordance with the Dutch IVF planning decree, every licensed IVF center and their corresponding transport and satellite centers must provide annual reports on treatment outcomes for uniform national IVF registration. LOE DMuGOrganization of fertility care (registration)
Patient-centered recommendations
Both partners of the couple should be involved in the assessment and management of infertility because it is a joint problem. LOE CMuGPhysician, gynecologist, and urologist (basic principles)
The gynecologist should offer couples with fertility problems psychological support throughout all phases of fertility care. LOE DMuGGynecologist (information provision)
Patient recommendations
Patients want their gynecologist to inform them about the different phases of treatment and their expected time spans. LOE PMuGGynecologist (information provision)
Patients want their physician to make a referral immediately after they have been trying to conceive for 1 year. LOE PMuGphysician (referral)

cells.cell_text: The physician should only physically examine the man if his semen analysis is irregular. LOE C
The gynecologist should not test ovarian reserve capacity to predict probability of pregnancy (with or without treatment). LOE A
The physician should order a semen analysis from an accredited laboratory (ISO15189) or from a referral hospital. LOE D

In accordance with the Dutch IVF planning decree, every licensed IVF center and their corresponding transport and satellite centers must provide annual reports on treatment outcomes for uniform national IVF registration. LOE D
Both partners of the couple should be involved in the assessment and management of infertility because it is a joint problem. LOE C
The gynecologist should offer couples with fertility problems psychological support throughout all phases of fertility care. LOE D
Patients want their gynecologist to inform them about the different phases of treatment and their expected time spans. LOE P
Patients want their physician to make a referral immediately after they have been trying to conceive for 1 year. LOE P

page.content: In accordance with the Dutch IVF planning decree, every licensed IVF center and their corresponding transport and satellite centers must provide annual reports on treatment outcomes for uniform national IVF registration, also classified as LOE D. Patient-centered recommendations emphasize that both partners of the couple should be involved in the assessment and management of infertility because it is a joint problem, noted as LOE C, and the gynecologist should offer couples with fertility problems psychological support throughout all phases of fertility care, categorized as LOE D. Regarding patient recommendations, patients want their gynecologist to inform them about the different phases of treatment and their expected time spans, indicated as LOE P, and they want their physician to make a referral immediately after they have been trying to conceive for 1 year, also classified as LOE P. The gynecologist's role in information provision is underscored in each of these recommendations, demonstrating the importance of comprehensive care in fertility management.

conceive for 1 year, also classified as LOE P. The gynecologist's role in information provision is underscored in each of these
recommendations, demonstrating the importance of comprehensive care in fertility management.
